violation_descriptions: furnace too close to wood framing
appliance clearance to combustibles insufficient
heater installed too close to combustible material
not enough clearance to combustible wall or ceiling
water heater clearance from wood less than required
plain_language_rule: Appliances must maintain minimum clearances to combustible materials as specified on the appliance nameplate or in code Table 4.1.

violation_descriptions: clearance to combustibles reduced without proper protection
heat shield not installed per Table 4.1
reduced clearance with no approved shielding
combustible protection not in accordance with code table
plain_language_rule: Clearances to combustibles shall not be reduced unless certified on the nameplate or protection is provided in accordance with Table 4.1.

violation_descriptions: appliance on combustible floor with no base protection
furnace sitting directly on plywood subfloor
no masonry base under appliance rated for noncombustible floor
missing floor protection under gas appliance
plain_language_rule: An appliance certified for noncombustible flooring on combustible flooring requires two courses of 4 in hollow masonry covered by sheet metal, extending 6 in beyond the appliance sides.

violation_descriptions: no service clearance around appliance
furnace installed too tight to wall for servicing
cannot access appliance for maintenance
service clearance less than 24 inches
insufficient space to remove components for repair
plain_language_rule: Appliances shall be installed with minimum 24 in (610 mm) service clearance to any side where service could be necessary.

violation_descriptions: access opening to furnace room too small
no access opening to appliance space
mechanical room door undersized
cannot get appliance in or out of room
plain_language_rule: An access opening with minimum dimensions of 24 x 30 in (610 x 760 mm) shall be provided to the space where an appliance is located.

violation_descriptions: passageway to appliance blocked or too narrow
clear path to furnace less than 36 inches
obstructed passageway to water heater
cannot reach appliance through crawlspace
plain_language_rule: A clear and unobstructed passageway at least 36 in (900 mm) high and 36 in (900 mm) wide shall be provided to each appliance.

violation_descriptions: no manual shutoff valve at appliance
appliance shutoff valve missing
gas valve not installed on drop to furnace
no individual shutoff for this appliance
shutoff valve more than 50 ft from residential appliance
plain_language_rule: A readily accessible manual shut-off valve for each appliance shall be installed in the drop or riser as close as possible to the appliance valve train.

violation_descriptions: sediment trap missing at appliance
no drip leg installed
dirt pocket not installed on gas drop
drip leg missing on furnace gas line
sediment trap not capped
plain_language_rule: A dirt pocket shall be installed at the bottom of piping on the final drop serving most appliances, connected through the bottom of a tee, capped, and accessible for cleaning.

violation_descriptions: drip leg too short
sediment trap depth less than 3 inches
dirt pocket undersized for pipe diameter
drip pocket does not meet minimum depth
plain_language_rule: Pocket depth shall be 3 in (75 mm) or equal to the pipe internal diameter, whichever is greater. Diameter shall be NPS 2 or equal to the piping diameter, whichever is less.

violation_descriptions: drip leg not connected through bottom of tee
sediment trap installed with incorrect fitting
dirt pocket not using tee configuration
drip leg installed sideways not at bottom of tee
plain_language_rule: When required, a drip or dirt pocket shall be connected through the bottom opening of a tee, the other two openings serving as continuity for the piping system.

violation_descriptions: vent connector too close to combustible wall
single wall vent pipe clearance less than 6 inches
B-vent connector clearance insufficient
flue pipe touching combustible material
vent connector clearance not per Table 8.6
plain_language_rule: Vent connector clearances per Table 8.6: Type B = 1 in (25 mm) for most appliances; single-wall = 6 in (150 mm) for most appliances.

violation_descriptions: vent connector passes through combustible wall without thimble
no ventilated thimble where flue pipe goes through wall
single wall vent through combustible partition unguarded
vent penetration through wood wall not properly protected
plain_language_rule: A single-wall vent connector through a combustible wall must be guarded with a ventilated metal thimble at least 4 in larger in diameter, or noncombustible insulation limiting surface temp to 194F (90C).

violation_descriptions: vent connector not secured with screws
flue pipe joints not fastened
vent pipe fell off draft hood
vent connector not attached to flue collar
slip joints not secured
plain_language_rule: A vent connector shall be firmly attached to the draft-hood outlet or flue collar by sheet metal screws, mechanical fasteners, or per manufacturer's instructions.

violation_descriptions: vent connector sagging between supports
flue pipe dipping downward
vent pipe not properly supported causing sag
horizontal vent run has visible dip or belly
plain_language_rule: A vent connector shall be installed without dips or sags, securely supported by noncombustible hangers.

violation_descriptions: horizontal vent connector run too long
flue pipe horizontal distance excessive
appliance too far from chimney
vent connector lateral not as short as practicable
plain_language_rule: The horizontal run of a vent connector shall be as short as practicable. The appliance shall be located as near the vent or chimney as practicable.

violation_descriptions: single wall vent connector passing through floor
single wall flue pipe goes through ceiling
single wall vent penetrating floor or ceiling
B-vent not used where vent passes through floor
plain_language_rule: A single-wall vent connector shall not pass through a floor or ceiling. Type B or Type L vent must be used.

violation_descriptions: single wall vent connector in unheated attic
vent connector in cold garage not Type B
vent passing through unheated space not double wall
single wall vent in crawlspace
plain_language_rule: When a vent connector from a Category I appliance passes through an unheated space, that portion shall be Type B or Type L vent.

violation_descriptions: barometric damper missing on oil-to-gas conversion
no draft regulator installed where required
barometric damper installed on appliance with draft hood
draft regulator on wrong appliance type
plain_language_rule: A draft regulator (barometric damper) stabilizes draft by admitting room air. It shall not be located where positive vent pressures occur in a special venting system.

violation_descriptions: no combustion air supply to furnace room
combustion air opening missing
sealed mechanical room with no outside air
tight construction with no air supply for appliances
plain_language_rule: An outdoor air supply shall be provided for appliances in tight-construction buildings with sealed vapour barriers or equivalent leakage area of 78 sq in or less at 10 Pa.

violation_descriptions: combustion air opening too small
air supply duct undersized for total BTU input
combustion air not sized per Table 8.1 or 8.2
free area of air supply insufficient
plain_language_rule: Free area of outdoor air supply shall be determined from Table 8.1 (draft-control) or Table 8.2 (no draft-control) based on total input of all appliances.

violation_descriptions: combustion air duct not terminating near burner level
air supply duct terminates too far from appliance
combustion air duct opening in wrong location
duct not within 1 ft above and 2 ft horizontal of burner
plain_language_rule: The combustion air duct shall terminate within 1 ft (300 mm) above and 2 ft (600 mm) horizontally from the burner level of the largest-input appliance.

violation_descriptions: combustion air inlet too close to ground
outside air opening below 12 inches above grade
air supply intake at grade level
inlet covered by snow or debris
plain_language_rule: An air-supply inlet opening from outdoors shall be located not less than 12 in (300 mm) above outside grade level.

violation_descriptions: combustion air opening within 3 ft of dryer exhaust
air supply too close to moisture exhaust duct
intake near clothes dryer vent discharge
combustion air inlet near spa exhaust
plain_language_rule: An air-supply opening shall not be located within 3 ft (0.9 m) of a moisture exhaust duct such as a clothes dryer discharge.

violation_descriptions: exhaust fan causing appliance backdraft
fireplace depressurizing furnace room
range hood pulling flue gases into home
negative pressure affecting gas appliance venting
plain_language_rule: When exhaust fans, fireplaces, or other equipment adversely affect venting or combustion, the condition shall be corrected or the fuel supply discontinued. See CSA F300.

violation_descriptions: gas piping not bonded
no bonding jumper on gas line
equipotential bonding conductor missing
gas pipe not electrically continuous
missing No. 6 AWG bonding wire
plain_language_rule: All building metal gas piping systems shall be electrically continuous and equipotentially bonded using No. 6 AWG copper or No. 4 AWG aluminum, accessible after installation.

violation_descriptions: CSST not bonded per manufacturer instructions
corrugated stainless steel tubing missing bonding
CSST without arc-resistant jacket not separately bonded
yellow flex gas line not bonded
plain_language_rule: CSST without an arc-resistant jacket shall be bonded per the CSST manufacturer's installation instructions, in addition to Clause 4.7 requirements.

violation_descriptions: gas connector too long
flexible connector passing through wall or floor
connector running room to room
gas connector not in same area as appliance
plain_language_rule: A gas connector shall not pass through a wall, floor, ceiling, or partition, and shall be connected to rigid piping in the same area as the appliance.

violation_descriptions: gas connector not certified
unapproved flexible connector used
dryer connected with uncertified hose
gas connector without CSA markings
plain_language_rule: A gas connector shall be certified to CSA/ANSI Z21.24/CSA 6.10, ANSI Z21.69/CSA 6.16, ANSI Z21.75/CSA 6.27, CSA/ANSI Z21.54/CSA 8.4, or CSA/ANSI Z21.101/CSA 8.5.

violation_descriptions: gas hose connected to vented appliance
rubber gas hose on furnace
gas hose used on water heater
gas hose on vented space heater
plain_language_rule: A gas hose connection to a vented appliance is prohibited except where specifically permitted.

violation_descriptions: no overpressure protection device on regulator
line relief valve missing
regulator without internal relief
overpressure device setpoint exceeds Table 5.2
plain_language_rule: A line or industrial pressure regulator shall have an overpressure protection device set to not exceed the maximum allowable downstream pressures in Table 5.2.

violation_descriptions: gas pressure at appliance too high
supply pressure exceeds appliance rating
manifold pressure not within nameplate range
gas pressure too low for proper operation
plain_language_rule: Gas shall be supplied at a pressure within the range on the rating plate or manufacturer's instructions, at max and min load demand.

violation_descriptions: regulator vent not pointing down
outdoor regulator bonnet vent facing upward
regulator vent allowing water entry
pressure regulator vent orientation incorrect
plain_language_rule: A pressure regulator installed outdoors or in unheated areas shall have its bonnet vent discharge vertically downward.

violation_descriptions: no shutoff valve upstream of pressure regulator
manual valve missing before regulator
regulator installed without upstream isolation valve
no valve before line pressure regulator
plain_language_rule: A pressure regulator shall have a manual shut-off valve installed upstream and an overpressure protection device per Clause 5.3.

violation_descriptions: appliance not listed or certified
unapproved gas appliance installed
no CSA or other certification label
appliance without rating plate
equipment not approved for intended use
plain_language_rule: An appliance, accessory, component, equipment, or material shall be of a type and rating approved for the specific purpose for which it is employed.

violation_descriptions: appliance connected to wrong type of gas
natural gas appliance on propane supply
propane appliance on natural gas
appliance not converted for fuel type
plain_language_rule: An appliance shall not be installed unless designed for the type of gas to which it is connected and suitable for the pressure supplied.

violation_descriptions: appliance converted without manufacturer instructions
gas conversion not marked on rating plate
field conversion not done per certified instructions
conversion orifices not correct size
plain_language_rule: When an appliance is converted from one gas to another, it shall be done per manufacturer's certified instructions. The converted gas shall be marked on the rating plate.

violation_descriptions: leak test not completed on new piping
no pressure test record
piping not tested before appliance connection
pressure test not performed per Table 6.3
plain_language_rule: Before appliance connection and before fuel gas is introduced, new gas piping shall be pressure tested using air or inert gas per Table 6.3.

violation_descriptions: no leak test after appliance reconnection
service leak check not performed
piping not tested after gas reintroduction
no 10 minute standing test at operating pressure
plain_language_rule: Before first activation or after gas reintroduction, downstream piping shall be tested for leaks by watching the meter test dial or pressure gauge for 10 minutes.

violation_descriptions: valve train connections not checked for leaks
no soap bubble test on gas connections
threaded connections not leak-checked
appliance fittings not tested at operating pressure
plain_language_rule: After the standing leak test, each appliance connection, valve, and valve train shall be checked for leaks at operating pressure with liquid solution or leak-detection device.

violation_descriptions: vent termination too low above roof
vent cap less than 2 ft above roof penetration
chimney not extending 3 ft above roof
vent not 2 ft above any point within 10 ft
plain_language_rule: A vent shall extend not less than 2 ft above the highest roof penetration point and 2 ft above any building portion within 10 ft horizontal. Chimneys require 3 ft above roof.

violation_descriptions: vent terminal too close to window
vent termination near air intake
vent cap below required distance from door
sidewall vent near openable window
plain_language_rule: Vent terminals require clearances from windows/doors per input: 6 in up to 10K BTU, 12 in to 100K, 3 ft over 100K. Also 6 ft from mechanical air intake, 1 ft above grade.

violation_descriptions: vent less than 5 ft above draft hood
chimney height less than 5 ft above flue collar
insufficient vent height above appliance
total vent rise less than minimum
plain_language_rule: A vent or chimney shall extend not less than 5 ft (1.5 m) in height above the highest connected appliance draft-hood outlet or flue collar.

violation_descriptions: safety shutoff valve bypassed
safety limit control disabled
relief valve rendered inoperative
safety device jumpered out or isolated
plain_language_rule: Isolating or rendering inoperative a safety shut-off valve, safety limit control, or relief valve is prohibited.

violation_descriptions: gas piping not properly supported
pipe hangers missing
gas line sagging between supports
piping not braced for expansion
plain_language_rule: Piping shall be mounted, braced, and supported to provide for expansion, contraction, jarring, vibration, and settling, protected against damage.

violation_descriptions: gas pipe used as electrical ground
electrical circuit run through gas piping
gas line used for grounding electrode
piping used in lieu of wiring
plain_language_rule: Piping shall not be used for an electrical ground. An electric circuit shall not utilize piping in lieu of wiring, except low-voltage control circuits as part of an appliance.

violation_descriptions: close nipple used in gas piping
street elbow in gas piping system
street tee used in gas line
bushings nested in gas piping
plain_language_rule: A close nipple, street elbow, or street tee shall not be used in a gas piping system. Bushings shall not be nested.

violation_descriptions: concealed gas piping with inaccessible joints
hidden gas fittings behind drywall not tested
gas joints concealed before pressure test
fittings in wall cavity not inspected
plain_language_rule: Concealed piping with fittings or joints shall not be run where those joints cannot be inspected and tested in final position prior to being concealed.

violation_descriptions: appliance in garage with ignition source below 18 inches
water heater burner too low in storage garage
garage appliance not FVIR and below 18 in
ignition source in repair garage below 4.5 ft
plain_language_rule: In a storage garage, unless FVIR-certified, appliance ignition components must be 18 in above floor. In a repair garage, 4.5 ft (1400 mm) above floor.

violation_descriptions: cracked heat exchanger not replaced
defective heat exchanger in dwelling unit furnace
heat exchanger with visible hole or crack
CO from cracked heat exchanger
plain_language_rule: Where the heat exchanger of a furnace in a dwelling unit is found defective, it shall be replaced. Temporary repair may be permitted per the authority having jurisdiction.

violation_descriptions: propane tank too close to building
tank setback from property line insufficient
propane tank under electrical line
tank within minimum distance of ignition source
plain_language_rule: Tanks over 2000 USWG shall not be under electrical transmission lines and must maintain minimum horizontal distances per code tables from distribution lines.

violation_descriptions: propane tank missing relief valve
tank PRD not properly sized
relief valve blocked or tampered with
shut-off valve installed between relief valve and tank
plain_language_rule: A tank shall have a properly sized spring-loaded relief valve per provincial requirements with direct communication to vapour space. No shutoff between relief valve and tank.

violation_descriptions: propane regulator vent not pointing down
outdoor propane regulator bonnet vent facing up
regulator in dome vent not angled down 15 degrees
propane regulator vent allowing moisture entry
plain_language_rule: An outdoor propane regulator shall have its bonnet vent discharge vertically downward. In a container dome, positioned downward not less than 15 degrees from horizontal.

violation_descriptions: propane cylinder purged too close to building
purging within 10 ft of building
purging near ignition source
cylinder purged near air intake
plain_language_rule: Cylinders shall not be purged within 10 ft (3 m) of a building, property line, source of ignition, or air intake of any appliance.

violation_descriptions: match used to check for propane leak
open flame used for leak detection
lighter used to find gas leak
candle used to check fittings for leaks
plain_language_rule: A match, candle, flame, or other source of ignition shall not be used to check for a propane leak.

violation_descriptions: propane not properly odourized
no odorant in propane supply
propane has no detectable smell
odourization not per CAN/CGSB-3.14
plain_language_rule: Propane distributed for fuel purposes shall be odourized in accordance with CAN/CGSB-3.14.

violation_descriptions: only single-stage regulator on permanent propane installation
missing second stage propane regulator
no two-stage regulation on propane system
single regulator on residential propane
plain_language_rule: Not less than two-stage regulation shall be utilized on all permanent propane installations.

violation_descriptions: installer left without verifying safe operation
appliance not checked before leaving job
installer did not confirm code compliance
appliance not activated and tested by installer
plain_language_rule: Installers shall ensure compliance with code requirements before leaving. The person activating the appliance shall ensure it is in safe working order.

violation_descriptions: no record of piping pressure test
installer did not perform pressure test
pressure test documentation missing
test not done per Clause 6.22.2
plain_language_rule: It is the responsibility of the installer to perform pressure tests per Clause 6.22.2 and ensure the piping system is gas-tight at completion.

violation_descriptions: propane cylinder missing OPD
BBQ tank has no overfill prevention device
old cylinder without OPD valve being refilled
20 lb cylinder missing overfill protection
plain_language_rule: Refillable vapour service cylinders (4 lb to 40 lb) manufactured after January 1, 2008 must have an overfill prevention device compliant with UL 2227, except cylinders in industrial truck service or identified for welding and cutting gases.

violation_descriptions: expired propane cylinder refilled
cylinder past requalification date
cylinder not recertified within 10 years
rusted or dented cylinder still in service
plain_language_rule: Refillable cylinders must be manufactured, tested, inspected, requalified, and marked in accordance with CSA B339. Non-refillable cylinders must meet TC 39M or TC 2P/2P1/2P2/2Q/2Q2/2R specifications.

violation_descriptions: fixed liquid level gauge bleeding excessive propane
bleed valve opening oversized on cylinder gauge
rotary gauge venting too much gas when checked
plain_language_rule: A liquid level gauge that bleeds propane to atmosphere (rotary, fixed-liquid-level, or slip-tube type) must have a bleed valve opening no larger than a No. 54 drill size (1.4 mm) unless equipped with an excess-flow valve.

violation_descriptions: propane regulator too small for connected load
appliances starving for gas at high fire
regulator cannot supply total BTU demand
low gas pressure when multiple appliances run
plain_language_rule: A pressure regulator must be sized to provide the required flow rate of propane at the extremes of inlet pressures to which the regulator can be exposed.

violation_descriptions: regulator hanging loose on pigtail
regulator not secured to container valve or bracket
regulator exposed to weather with no protection
regulator vent facing up collecting water
plain_language_rule: A pressure regulator must be firmly secured to the container valve, a wall or container dome bracket, or otherwise secured. Outdoor regulators subject to inclement weather must be protected from weather exposure.

violation_descriptions: propane tank exposed to vehicle traffic
no bollards around propane container in parking lot
cylinder storage with no guardrail or post protection
tank beside driveway could be struck by vehicle
plain_language_rule: Containers installed or stored where they are not protected from motor vehicle damage (street, highway, alley, parking lot) must be protected by posts or guardrails per Clause 7.19.4 unless otherwise approved by the authority having jurisdiction.

violation_descriptions: weeds and dry grass around propane tank
garbage or debris stored against propane cylinder
long grass within 10 feet of container
firewood stacked beside propane tank
plain_language_rule: Readily ignitable materials, including weeds and long dry grass, must be removed within 10 ft (3 m) of a container and the area kept clear at all times.

violation_descriptions: disconnected propane container valve not plugged
open service valve outlet on tank left on site
distributor tank disconnected with unplugged outlets
plain_language_rule: When a distributor-owned container is disconnected by the distributor and left on a customer's property, all container service valve outlets must be plugged.

violation_descriptions: filling propane cylinder inside a building
cylinder filled near appliance air intake
filling within 10 feet of ignition source
container gauged in poorly ventilated area
plain_language_rule: A container must not be filled or gauged inside a building (unless designed for that purpose), in a poorly ventilated location, within 10 ft (3 m) horizontally of any appliance or air-moving equipment air intake, or within 10 ft (3 m) of any ignition source.

violation_descriptions: propane transfer left unattended
driver walked away during tank filling
cylinder filling with nobody at the container
plain_language_rule: While propane is being transferred from one container to another, the person transferring must remain at the container being filled.

violation_descriptions: uncertified person filling propane cylinders
employee without propane certificate dispensing
no record of training for propane transfer
plain_language_rule: Propane may only be transferred from one container to another by a person holding a certificate recognized by the authority having jurisdiction.

violation_descriptions: propane cylinder overfilled
tank filled past maximum filling density
container filled beyond 80 percent
liquid propane at relief valve after fill
plain_language_rule: The person filling a container is responsible for ensuring the maximum permitted filling density is not exceeded.

violation_descriptions: new propane container not purged before filling
air left in new cylinder before first fill
container opened to atmosphere then filled without purging
plain_language_rule: A new container, or one from which the propane atmosphere has been removed, must be purged before filling.

violation_descriptions: appliance exposed to fire reconnected to supply
flood damaged furnace put back in service
appliance from flooded basement reused without inspection
fire damaged equipment reactivated
plain_language_rule: An appliance exposed to fire, explosion, flood, or other damage must not be sold, installed, reactivated, or reconnected to supply until inspected by a person acceptable to the authority having jurisdiction.

violation_descriptions: natural gas appliance connected to propane
appliance not converted for propane use
wrong orifice size for gas type
appliance not rated for supplied pressure
plain_language_rule: An appliance must not be installed unless it is designed for the type of gas to which it will be connected and is suitable for the pressure supplied.

violation_descriptions: uncertified appliance installed on propane
no certification label on equipment
unapproved component used in propane system
imported appliance without Canadian approval
plain_language_rule: Any appliance, accessory, component, equipment, or material used in an installation must be of a type and rating approved for the specific purpose for which it is employed.

violation_descriptions: propane piping not pressure tested
no test performed on tubing system before activation
system activated without gas-tight verification
plain_language_rule: The installer of a piping or tubing system is responsible for performing pressure tests in accordance with Clause 6.22.2 of CSA B149.1 and ensuring the system is gas-tight at completion of the tests.

violation_descriptions: appliance installed in corrosive atmosphere
propane heater in pool chemical storage room
unit installed where chlorine vapours present
plain_language_rule: An appliance must not be installed in a room with an atmosphere containing vapours corrosive to an appliance or venting system.

violation_descriptions: safety shutoff valve jumpered out
limit control bypassed
relief device plugged or rendered inoperative
safety control wired around
plain_language_rule: Isolating or rendering inoperative a safety shut-off valve, safety limit control, or relief device is prohibited.

violation_descriptions: smoking while working on propane piping
ignition source in propane work area
open flame near equipment containing propane
plain_language_rule: Smoking or any other ignition source is not permitted where work is being done on piping, tubing, or equipment that contains or has contained propane, unless it has been purged of all propane per Clause 6.23 of CSA B149.1.

violation_descriptions: appliance has no service clearance
cannot access unit for servicing
less than 24 inches clearance on serviceable side
appliance boxed in by construction
plain_language_rule: An appliance must have a minimum service clearance of 24 in (61 cm) from any side, top, or bottom where service could be necessary, or more where the rating plate requires it or component removal demands it.

violation_descriptions: propane tank rusted and not painted
bare steel showing on tank surface
tank coating failed with corrosion visible
rusty unpainted propane tank
plain_language_rule: Every steel tank must be kept painted.

violation_descriptions: tank relief valve missing rain cap
relief valve open to weather
no protective cap on tank PRV
plain_language_rule: A loose-fitting rain cap must be provided for all tank relief valves.

violation_descriptions: direct fired vaporizer installed inside building
vaporizer in pumphouse or filling room
open flame in building housing vaporizer
plain_language_rule: Subject to Clause 9.3.4, a direct-fired vaporizer is not permitted in a pumphouse, container filling or storage room, or any other building or room. Open flames are prohibited in buildings housing vaporizers unless the vaporizer is direct-fired.

violation_descriptions: gas line through exterior wall not sleeved
tubing through wall to unheated space without sleeve
pipe through exterior wall not wrapped or sealed
plain_language_rule: A sleeve must enclose the entire length of piping or tubing passing through an exterior wall to an unheated, inaccessible building element, sealed watertight, with the piping or tubing inside the sleeve double wrapped with pipe wrap tape.

violation_descriptions: gas meter exposed to vehicle damage
riser in parking lot with no bollards
above grade gas piping unprotected from vehicles
regulator beside alley with no posts or guardrail
plain_language_rule: Above-grade gas piping in locations not protected from vehicle damage (street, highway, alley, parking lot) must be protected by posts or guardrails meeting Clause 6.16.16 unless otherwise approved by the authority having jurisdiction.

violation_descriptions: purging gas piping indoors improperly
venting gas inside building during purge
purge discharge point not monitored
no combustible gas indicator during indoor purge
plain_language_rule: Indoor purging is only permitted under Clause 6.23.6 conditions: ignite the gas at an appliance up to 400,000 Btu/h with a readily accessible burner and continuous ignition source, or follow Annex B for appliances without a continuous pilot. Any open discharge point must be continuously attended by a qualified person and monitored with a combustible gas indicator.

violation_descriptions: water heater installed in bedroom not direct vent
tank water heater in bathroom
water heater in room with sleeping accommodation
plain_language_rule: A water heater must not be installed in a bathroom, bedroom, or any enclosure where sleeping accommodation is provided, unless it is of the direct-vent type.

violation_descriptions: room heater in bathroom not direct vent
space heater installed in bathroom drawing room air
plain_language_rule: A room heater in a bathroom must be of the direct-vent type.

violation_descriptions: gas fireplace in bedroom not direct vent
decorative appliance installed in sleeping room
fireplace in bathroom without direct vent certification
plain_language_rule: A decorative appliance must not be installed in a bathroom or sleeping room unless certified to ANSI Z21.50/CSA 2.22 and direct-vent type, or (for bedrooms/bed-sitting rooms) marked as also certified for bedroom installation.

violation_descriptions: return air inlet in space supplying combustion air
return duct opening in furnace enclosure
return air pulled from crawl space providing combustion air
plain_language_rule: Return-air inlets must not be installed in an enclosure or crawl space that provides combustion air to a furnace.

violation_descriptions: regulator vent missing insect screen
vent line termination open to rain and debris
relief vent termination has no protection against water entry
plain_language_rule: The outdoor vent line termination of a pressure regulator or line relief device must be equipped with a means to prevent the entry of water, insects, or foreign material.
